title: Coussarea odoratissima
taxonomy: Coussarea odoratissima is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae families. The Rubiaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with a large number of species found in Central and South America. Coussarea odoratissima is native to Central and South America, particularly in the tropical forests of these regions. Within the Rubiaceae family, Coussarea odoratissima is part of the tribe Cinchoneae, a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the cinchona tree.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
